Caption: Illustration of the proposed C-rate-dependent RDE definition for LiBs. The shaded areas represent the RDE at different discharging C-rate levels. The present time is t and the remaining discharge time is Δ t RDT .

Variation of the RDE of an NCA cell across different C-rates under a modified US06 discharging profile, with V min = 3 V , T max = 50 ° C , and T amb = 25 ° C . (a) E RDE subject to the limits of V min and T max ; (b) E RDE subject to only the limit of V min ; (c) difference of E RDE between (a) and (b). The results show the C-rate dependence of a cell’s RDE and demonstrate the effect of T max limit for RDE prediction at high discharging C-rates. When only considering V min limit, the cell’s temperature reaches a maximum of 67 . 5 ° C during the prediction of E RDE at z = 8 C and t = 0 s .
Diagrams of (a) the NDC model and (b) the lumped thermal model.

(a) The NDCTNet model, which integrates physics with ML to predict the terminal voltage and temperature of a LiB cell, see Section 3; (b) the pipeline chart of the proposed RDE prediction approach, see Section 4.
Bisection search for Δ t RDT T max .
Validation of the NDCTNet model for the NCA cell based on (a) the modified US06 (0 ∼ 8 C) and (b) the modified SC04 (0 ∼ 8 C) profiles.
Validation of the NDCTNet model for the LFP cell based on (a) the modified US06 (0 ∼ 15 C) and (b) the modified SC04 (0 ∼ 15 C) profiles.

RDT prediction results for the NCA cell under (a) the modified US06 (0 ∼ 8 C) and (b) the modified SC04 (0 ∼ 8 C) testing profiles at T amb = 25 ° C . The inset plots highlight the RDT at high C-rate loads.

RDT prediction results for the LFP cell under (a) the modified US06 (0 ∼ 15 C) and (b) the modified SC04 (0 ∼ 15 C) testing profiles at T amb = 25 ° C . The inset plots highlight the RDT at high C-rate loads.
